name: Quantum Materials
description: Study of materials where quantum mechanical effects manifest at the macroscopic scale, giving rise to emergent phenomena such as superconductivity, topological order, and quantum spin liquids.

name: Strongly Correlated Systems
description: Systems where electron-electron interactions dominate, leading to phenomena beyond single-particle band theory: Mott insulators, heavy fermions, non-Fermi liquid behavior, and emergent orders.

name: Topological Materials
description: Materials with topologically non-trivial electronic band structures, including topological insulators, Weyl/Dirac semimetals, nodal-line semimetals, and topological superconductors.

name: 2D Materials
description: Atomically thin layered materials exhibiting novel electronic, optical, and mechanical properties: graphene, transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDs), hexagonal boron nitride, and van der Waals heterostructures.

name: Superconductors
description: Materials exhibiting zero electrical resistance below a critical temperature; encompassing conventional (BCS), unconventional (cuprates, iron-based, heavy fermion), and topological superconductors.

name: Surface Science
description: Study of physical and chemical phenomena at solid surfaces and interfaces using photoemission and complementary techniques: surface states, adsorbates, catalysis, and thin film growth.

name: High-Tc Cuprate Superconductors
description: Copper-oxide based superconductors with high critical temperatures; study of pseudogap phase, strange metal behavior, charge density wave order, and the mechanism of unconventional superconductivity.

name: Iron-Based Superconductors
description: Iron-pnictide and iron-chalcogenide superconductors discovered in 2008; electronic structure, gap symmetry, nematic order, orbital-selective physics, and the interplay between magnetism and superconductivity.

name: Heavy Fermion Materials
description: Intermetallic compounds with f-electron elements (Ce, Yb, U) showing extremely large effective electron masses; quantum criticality, unconventional superconductivity, and Kondo physics.

name: Topological Insulators
description: Bulk-insulating materials with topologically protected conducting surface/edge states (e.g., Bi2Se3, Bi2Te3, Sb2Te3); spin-momentum locking, quantum anomalous Hall effect, and axion insulator physics.

name: Weyl and Dirac Semimetals
description: Three-dimensional topological semimetals with band-touching points (Weyl/Dirac nodes) in the bulk; Fermi arc surface states, chiral anomaly, and large magnetoresistance.

name: Graphene
description: Single-layer carbon atoms arranged in a honeycomb lattice; Dirac cone electronic structure, massless Dirac fermions, twistronics, and correlated phases in magic-angle bilayer graphene.

name: Transition Metal Dichalcogenides (TMDs)
description: Layered materials of the form MX2 (M=Mo,W; X=S,Se,Te); valleytronics, Ising superconductivity, charge density waves, and Mott insulating phases in TMD heterostructures.

name: Charge Density Wave Systems
description: Materials exhibiting periodic modulation of electron density coupled to lattice distortion; interplay of CDW order with superconductivity, Mott physics, and topological states.

name: Kagome Lattice Materials
description: Materials with kagome lattice geometry hosting flat bands, Dirac cones, and van Hove singularities; frustrated magnetism, charge order, and unconventional superconductivity (e.g., AV3Sb5 family).

name: ARPES Instrumentation and Methodology
description: Development of advanced ARPES techniques: spin-resolved ARPES, time-resolved ARPES, micro/nano-ARPES, laser-based ARPES, in-situ combined systems (MBE-ARPES), and data analysis methods.

name: Pseudogap in Cuprates
description: The enigmatic partial suppression of electronic density of states above Tc in underdoped cuprates; competing order scenarios, Fermi arc phenomenology, and preformed pairs.

name: Nematic Order in Iron-Based Superconductors
description: Spontaneous breaking of rotational symmetry in iron-based superconductors; electronic nematicity, its relationship to orbital order, and coupling to the lattice.

name: Quantum Spin Liquids
description: Exotic magnetic ground states with long-range entanglement and fractionalized excitations; experimental signatures in ARPES and spectroscopic probes of candidate materials.

name: Twistronics and Moire Materials
description: Electronic structure engineering through rotational stacking of 2D materials; magic-angle graphene, moire excitons, and correlated insulating/superconducting states in moire superlattices.

name: Time-Resolved ARPES (trARPES)
description: Ultrafast pump-probe ARPES to study non-equilibrium electronic dynamics; electron-phonon coupling dynamics, Floquet engineering, and photoinduced phase transitions on femtosecond timescales.

name: Nickel-Based Superconductors
description: Recently discovered infinite-layer nickelate superconductors (Nd1-xSrxNiO2); electronic structure comparison with cuprates and search for high-Tc superconductivity in nickel oxides.

name: Antiferromagnetic Topological Insulators
description: Intrinsic antiferromagnetic topological insulators (e.g., MnBi2Te4); axion insulator state, quantized magnetoelectric effect, and layered AFM topological phases.
